title: Sprint 9: Float64 Compatibility + NPT 20ps Gate + MTT Theory Foundation
problem_statement: Three blocking issues prevent v1.0 progression: (1) flash_nonbonded.py has four clusters of jnp.float32() literals that force computation into float32, breaking float64 simulations (lines 187, 231-236, 366, 456-462); (2) NPT 20ps gate still lacks a validated two-phase NVT→NPT protocol at liquid water density; (3) MTT log-det estimator is unblocked but mtt_theory.md design doc is missing, blocking implementation.

steps.description: Fix float32 literal in Born radii padding sanitization at flash_nonbonded.py:187. Replace `jnp.float32(1.5)` with plain Python float `1.5` (auto-promotes to positions.dtype). IMPORTANT: first grep for `dtype=jnp.float32` in the file — those are dtype arguments and must NOT be replaced; only bare `jnp.float32(<literal>)` calls should be changed. Also scan lines 180-195 for any other float32 literals.
steps.rationale: Surgical single-site fix. Fixer specializes in precision-aware code changes.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no bare jnp.float32(<number>) in lines 180-195; dtype= arguments preserved

steps.description: Fix float32 literals in soft-core + GB prefactor calculation at flash_nonbonded.py:231-236. Replace `jnp.float32(soft_core_lambda)`, `jnp.float32(0.5)`, `jnp.float32(-0.5)`, `jnp.float32(COULOMB_CONSTANT)`, `jnp.float32(tau)`, `jnp.float32(1e-8)` with plain Python literals. GB energy must accumulate in positions.dtype. Apply grep guard from Step 1: preserve any `dtype=jnp.float32` arguments.
steps.rationale: HIGH severity: GB energy forced to float32 breaks physics fidelity. chunked_fused_energy is the core energy accumulator.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no bare jnp.float32(<number>) in lines 225-245

steps.description: Fix float32 literals in _sparse_exclusion_energy at flash_nonbonded.py:366-368. Replace `jnp.float32(soft_core_lambda)` and `jnp.float32(0.5)` with plain Python floats. Apply grep guard.
steps.rationale: Exclusion correction computed in float32, subtracted from float64 total — precision loss at each correction.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no bare jnp.float32(<number>) in lines 360-375

steps.description: Fix float32 literals in parameter sanitization at flash_nonbonded.py:456-462 (flash_nonbonded_forces). Replace jnp.float32(1.5), jnp.float32(1.2), jnp.float32(0.0), jnp.float32(1.0) with plain Python floats. These sanitize padded atom parameters — forcing float32 here poisons all downstream energy computation. Apply grep guard.
steps.rationale: HIGH severity: sanitized parameters passed to energy_fn; float32 at this entry point propagates through entire nonbonded pipeline.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no bare jnp.float32(<number>) in lines 450-470

steps.description: Write regression test tests/physics/test_flash_nonbonded_dtype.py. Test parametrized over [jnp.float32, jnp.float64]: (a) construct 8-atom water positions at target dtype, (b) call flash_nonbonded_forces, (c) assert output dtype == input dtype, (d) assert energy is finite. Also test float32→float64 gives consistent energy values within 1e-4 relative tolerance. Mark float64 tests as slow.
steps.rationale: Regression gate to prevent future float32 regressions. Written by fixer immediately after fixes.
steps.gate: pytest tests/physics/test_flash_nonbonded_dtype.py -v exits 0; all parametrized variants pass

steps.description: Run existing flash_nonbonded test suite after fixes: `pytest tests/physics/test_flash_nonbonded*.py -v`. Verify zero regressions from float32→plain-float replacement.
steps.rationale: Confirm fixes do not alter float32 behavior — plain Python floats auto-promote to float32 when array is float32.
steps.gate: pytest exit code 0; all previously-passing tests still pass

steps.description: Implement two-phase NPT test at tests/physics/test_npt_barostat.py (add test_npt_20ps_liquid_water, marked slow): (1) _grid_water_positions(n_waters=64, spacing_angstrom=3.1) for liquid TIP3P density (~42 Å box), (2) settle.get_water_indices(0, 64), (3) NVT phase: settle_langevin init_fn+apply_fn for 4000 steps at dt=0.5fs (2 ps) to relax grid bad contacts — MUST be at least 4000 steps per oracle mandate, (4) construct NPTState from NVT final state + box, (5) settle_csvr_npt for 40000 steps (20ps) at P=1 bar, tau_thermostat=2000 AKMA, tau_barostat=2000 AKMA. Record (T, P, box_volume) every 200 steps.
steps.rationale: Complex integration test requiring careful state management across two integrators. 4000-step NVT ensures bad contacts from 3.1 Å grid spacing are relaxed before barostat engages.
steps.gate: 40000 NPT steps complete without NaN/exception; timeseries arrays have shape (200, 3)

steps.description: Validate NPT 20ps trajectory thermodynamics: (a) T_mean over last 10ps (steps 20000-40000) in [295, 305] K, (b) P_mean over last 10ps in [-99, 101] bar (±100 bar gate — consistent with Sprint 8 precedent for small explicit-water boxes; 64-water σ_P ~500-800 bar instantaneous, ~80-130 bar on 10ps mean), (c) no NaN in timeseries, (d) box volume varies smoothly — no sudden jumps >5%. Encode as pytest assertions in test_npt_20ps_liquid_water with docstring documenting ±100 bar rationale.
steps.rationale: Oracle-mandated thermodynamic gate. ±100 bar is physically justified for 64-water box (Sprint 8 precedent: 16 waters → ±100 bar). Auditor verifies assertions are correct.
steps.gate: test_npt_20ps_liquid_water PASSES all 4 assertions; P gate documented as ±100 bar with pressure fluctuation rationale in test docstring

steps.description: Write references/notes/mtt_theory.md algorithm design document covering all 7 sections: (1) Problem: stochastic log-det of Coulomb-weighted graph Laplacian L = D - K where K_ij = efa_lebedev_coulomb_energy kernel, (2) Hutchinson trace estimator: E[z^T M^{-1} z] = tr(M^{-1}) for z~N(0,I), (3) Lanczos tridiagonalization with JAX lax.while_loop implementation sketch — include re-orthogonalization strategy and stability note, (4) Chebyshev polynomial approximation of log via spectral density — include domain mapping for λ_min near zero (shift/regularization required), (5) Connection to EFA: efa_lebedev_coulomb_energy is the kernel function for K, reference efa_coulomb.py:209, (6) Error bounds: expected K (Hutchinson probes) and M (polynomial degree) for <5% error on 256-atom box — note N=256 is the milestone but Sprint 9 validates on N=64, (7) Dense eigendecomposition baseline (numpy.linalg.slogdet) for test validation.
steps.rationale: Theory synthesis must precede implementation. Sections 3 and 4 gate the Lanczos stability and Chebyshev domain choices in Step 10.
steps.gate: File exists at references/notes/mtt_theory.md with all 7 sections; sections 3 and 4 include explicit re-orthogonalization strategy and domain shift for near-zero eigenvalues

steps.description: Implement MTT log-det estimator at src/prolix/physics/mtt_logdet.py following mtt_theory.md design: (1) mtt_logdet_params(efa_params, n_probes, n_lanczos) -> MTTParams [offline], (2) mtt_estimate_log_det(positions, charges, atom_mask, mtt_params) -> scalar [online, JIT-able via functools.partial], (3) Lanczos via lax.while_loop with re-orthogonalization per theory doc, (4) Chebyshev approx of log eigenvalues with domain shift. Write tests/physics/test_mtt_logdet.py with three cases: (a) N=8 smoke test: JIT compiles, deterministic with same seed, (b) N=64 validation: <5% error vs numpy.linalg.slogdet on 64-atom Coulomb matrix [REQUIRED accuracy gate], (c) N=256 is the milestone target — explicitly defer to Sprint 10 with a skip marker in the test.
steps.rationale: Greenfield JAX numerical implementation. N=8 smoke + N=64 accuracy gate provides meaningful validation while deferring full 256-atom milestone.
steps.gate: pytest tests/physics/test_mtt_logdet.py passes N=8 smoke and N=64 <5% error; N=256 test present and marked skip with comment 'Sprint 10 milestone'

oracle_revision_notes: R1: NVT ≥4000 steps; R2: P gate ±100 bar; R3: N=64 MTT accuracy gate + skip N=256; R4: dtype= argument guard in float32 fix steps
